steps.title: Spot the behavioral contrast
steps.content: The decisive clue is that crows protect hooked tools—holding them in claws or storing them carefully—but don't do the same with basic tools.

steps.title: Infer what the protection reveals
steps.content: When one tool is protected and another isn't, that signals which is more valuable to the crows. Choice A directly states this inference. The other choices fail to explain the protective difference (D), mistake careful storage for ease of handling (B), or introduce sharing behavior the text never mentions (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
